description: Literature terms without accepted matches in their preferred ontologies.

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'abundance'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

candidate_matches.label: cartilaginous
candidate_matches.definition: Cartilage is a type of dense connective tissue. It is composed of specialized cells called chondrocytes (CL:0000138) that produce a large amount of extracellular matrix composed of collagen fibers, abundant ground substance rich in proteoglycan, and elastin fibers.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: addition of native seed effectively increased both the richness and abundance of native species

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: plots that received applications of grass-specific herbicide had lower abundances of exotic grasses than the remaining disturbance treatments

occurrences.title: Evaluating the efficacy of seasonal grazing and livestock exclusion as restoration tools for birds in riparian habitat of the Okanagan Valley, British Columbia, Canada
occurrences.evidence_text: abundance of W. Yellow-breasted Chats significantly increased

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental process corresponding to 'brood parasitism by Brown-headed Cowbirds'.

occurrences.title: Evaluating the efficacy of seasonal grazing and livestock exclusion as restoration tools for birds in riparian habitat of the Okanagan Valley, British Columbia, Canada
occurrences.evidence_text: brood parasitism by Brown-headed Cowbirds

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental process corresponding to 'livestock grazing'.

candidate_matches.label: tended pasturable land
candidate_matches.definition: Pasturable land which is maintained by human activities in order to provide grazing oppoturnities for livestock.

candidate_matches.label: area of pastureland or hayfields
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 20% of these areas are covered by pasturelands or hayfields.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition. The NLCD also notes that the crops are grown on a perennial cycle in these areas.

candidate_matches.label: ranch
candidate_matches.definition: An area of land used for the herding and grazing of livestock.

candidate_matches.label: rangeland biome
candidate_matches.definition: A rangeland biome is an anthropogenic terrestrial biome which is primarily used for the rearing and grazing of livestock.

candidate_matches.label: pasture
candidate_matches.definition: A grassland ecosystem which is used for grazing of ungulate livestock as part of a farm or ranch.

candidate_matches.label: agricultural field
candidate_matches.definition: A field which is located on land and used for agricultural purposes, such as the grazing of livestock or the cultivation of crops.

candidate_matches.label: agriculture
candidate_matches.definition: A land use process during which terrestrial environments are modified such that they can grow crop plants or allow the rearing of animals to provide food, fiber, medicines, or other products used by humans.

candidate_matches.label: hay
candidate_matches.definition: Hay is grass, legumes or other herbaceous plants that have been cut, dried, and stored for use as animal fodder, particularly for grazing livestock such as cattle, horses, goats, and sheep.

candidate_matches.label: farm
candidate_matches.definition: An area of land which is used for the cultivation of crops or grazing of livestock, including any agricultural constructions therein.

occurrences.evidence_text: livestock grazing

occurrences.title: Distinguishing reintroduction from recolonization with genetic testing
occurrences.evidence_text: Natural recolonization is possible in many areas that demonstrate some form of contemporary, or importantly future, functional connectivity to adjacent populations

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental process corresponding to 'population spread'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: there is evidence that all eight species colonized new plots since the last round of monitoring... at least 25% of the currently occupied plots are a result of population spread

occurrences.evidence_text: Instead it appears that recolonization by Albertan fishers is responsible for the current CLM population.

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'ER'.

candidate_matches.label: erosion
candidate_matches.definition: An action of exogenic processes (such as water flow or wind) which remove environmental material from one location on the surface of an astronomical body, transporting it to another location where it is deposited.

candidate_matches.label: soil erosion
candidate_matches.definition: Soil erosion is a process in which exogenic processes remove soil from one location on the Earth's crust and transport it to another location where it is deposited.

candidate_matches.label: erosional cave
candidate_matches.definition: A cave formed by erosion caused by flowing streams carrying rocks or sediments.

candidate_matches.label: effusive eruption
candidate_matches.definition: An effusive eruption is a volcanic eruption during which lava flows onto the ground.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: ER increased as GEPmax became more negative

occurrences.evidence_text: Natural recolonization is possible in many areas that demonstrate some form of contemporary, or importantly future, functional connectivity to adjacent populations

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'GEP max'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: GEPmax became more negative (greater CO2 uptake) in plots with higher moss cover across the bog sites

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'Methane flux'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Methane flux was related to water table position at both sites, with the flux increasing with shallower water table

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'moss cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of moss-dominated vegetation
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 80% of these areas are covered by mosses.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition. The original definition notes that this is intended as an "Alaska only" category.

candidate_matches.label: tundra ecosystem
candidate_matches.definition: Tundra ecosysems are often underlaid by permafrost, with the result that drainage is bad and the soil may be saturated for long periods. It does not have a permanent snow-ice cover.  Productivity is low in this biome because of the extremes of climate.

candidate_matches.label: cloud forest
candidate_matches.definition: A montane forest ecosystem which 1) is evergreen, 2) present on mountains where there is frequent cloud and mist cover, 3) which has a large proportion of ferns and mosses in its plant communities, growing on all tree trunks and branches present in the ecosystem, and 3) harvests aerosolised water and adds it to the terrestrial water supply.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: decreased with increasing ... moss cover ... at the fen site

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'native species abundance'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: addition of native seed effectively increased both the richness and abundance of native species

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'native species richness'.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: We found that plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'NEE max'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Higher sedge cover resulted in more negative NEE max values

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'over-confidence in past reintroductions'.

occurrences.title: Distinguishing reintroduction from recolonization with genetic testing
occurrences.evidence_text: over-confidence in past reintroductions, which might lead to significant expenditure of financial and human capital

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'restoring lost biodiversity'.

occurrences.title: Distinguishing reintroduction from recolonization with genetic testing
occurrences.evidence_text: Reintroductions are a common tool for restoring lost biodiversity around the globe and across taxa.

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'sedge cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of sedge- and forb-dominated herbaceous vegetation
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 80% of these areas are covered by sedge or forbs.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition. The NLCD description also notes that land covered by sedge tundra and sedge tussock tundra are subsumed in this category. The original definition notes that this is intended as an "Alaska only" category.

candidate_matches.label: tundra ecosystem
candidate_matches.definition: Tundra ecosysems are often underlaid by permafrost, with the result that drainage is bad and the soil may be saturated for long periods. It does not have a permanent snow-ice cover.  Productivity is low in this biome because of the extremes of climate.

candidate_matches.label: area of dwarf scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of dwarf scrub are dominated by shrubs less than 20 centimeters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This type is often co-associated with grasses, sedges, herbs, and non-vascular vegetation. The NLCD 2011 notes that this is intended as an Alaska only land cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: tundra mire
candidate_matches.definition: Peatlands with a shallow peat layer, only about 500 mm thick, dominated by sedges and grasses. They form in permafrost areas, covering around 110,000 to 160,000 km2 in Alaska, Canada, and the former USSR.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: Methane flux increased with increasing sedge cover at the bog site

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'shrub cover'.

candidate_matches.label: scrubland area
candidate_matches.definition: Area covered with low-growing or stunted perennial vegetation and usually not mixed with trees.

candidate_matches.label: tundra ecosystem
candidate_matches.definition: Tundra ecosysems are often underlaid by permafrost, with the result that drainage is bad and the soil may be saturated for long periods. It does not have a permanent snow-ice cover.  Productivity is low in this biome because of the extremes of climate.

candidate_matches.label: area of scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of scrub or shrub are dominated by shrubs; less than 5 meters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This class includes true shrubs, young trees in an early successional stage or trees stunted from environmental conditions. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of dwarf scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of dwarf scrub are dominated by shrubs less than 20 centimeters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This type is often co-associated with grasses, sedges, herbs, and non-vascular vegetation. The NLCD 2011 notes that this is intended as an Alaska only land cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of woody wetland
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, at least 20% of these areas are covered by forest or shrubland vegetation.  Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not strictly asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: steppe
candidate_matches.definition: A plain without trees (apart from those near rivers and lakes); it is similar to a prairie, although a prairie is generally considered as being dominated by tall grasses, while short grasses are said to be normal in the steppe. It may be semi-desert, or covered with grass or shrubs or both, depending on the season and latitude.

candidate_matches.label: woodland area
candidate_matches.definition: Land having a cover of trees, shrubs, or both.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: decreased with increasing shrub cover ... at the fen site

gaps.term: significant expenditure of financial and human capital
gaps.proposed_label: significant expenditure of financial and human capital

occurrences.evidence_text: over-confidence in past reintroductions, which might lead to significant expenditure of financial and human capital

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'soil temperature at 5 cm'.

occurrences.title: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration
occurrences.evidence_text: greater CO2 uptake through NEE max with ... higher soil temperature at 5 cm

gaps.proposed_definition: A environmental variable corresponding to 'total vegetation cover'.

candidate_matches.label: area of evergreen forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of evergreen forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that more than 75% of these trees should maintain their foliage despite seasonal change - the canopy is never without green foliage. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of dwarf scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of dwarf scrub are dominated by shrubs less than 20 centimeters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This type is often co-associated with grasses, sedges, herbs, and non-vascular vegetation. The NLCD 2011 notes that this is intended as an Alaska only land cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of scrub
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of scrub or shrub are dominated by shrubs; less than 5 meters tall with shrub canopy typically greater than 20% of total vegetation. This class includes true shrubs, young trees in an early successional stage or trees stunted from environmental conditions. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of mixed forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of mixed forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that neither deciduous or evergreen trees make up more than 75% of the total tree cover. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

candidate_matches.label: area of deciduous forest
candidate_matches.definition: According to the NLCD 2011, areas of deciduous forest are dominated by trees generally greater than 5 meters tall, and making up more than 20% of total vegetation cover. The NLCD 2011 continues to state that more than 75% of these trees should shed foliage simultaneously in response to seasonal change. Such thresholds - which are themselves fuzzy - are not asserted in this definition.

occurrences.evidence_text: decreased with increasing ... total cover ... at the fen site

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'cover'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

candidate_matches.label: setose
candidate_matches.definition: Setose as a label is used extensively to describe insect phenotypes that have hairy-like appearances (i.e., they are covered in setae, not the hairs of mammals). A bristle is a type of seta.

candidate_matches.label: pubescent hair
candidate_matches.definition: A pilosity quality of being covered with short hairs or soft down.

candidate_matches.label: wet
candidate_matches.definition: A wetness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered by a liquid.

candidate_matches.label: dry
candidate_matches.definition: A wetness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being covered by a liquid.

candidate_matches.label: scaly
candidate_matches.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered or partially covered with scales.

candidate_matches.label: hairy
candidate_matches.definition: A pilosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with hair or hairlike projections.

candidate_matches.label: warty
candidate_matches.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with warts or projections that resemble warts resulting in a hard rough surface.

candidate_matches.label: wetness
candidate_matches.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer's being covered by a liquid.

candidate_matches.label: cartilaginous
candidate_matches.definition: Cartilage is a type of dense connective tissue. It is composed of specialized cells called chondrocytes (CL:0000138) that produce a large amount of extracellular matrix composed of collagen fibers, abundant ground substance rich in proteoglycan, and elastin fibers.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: We found that plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded

gaps.proposed_definition: A variable corresponding to 'spread'.
gaps.candidate_parent: ELMO environmental variable or PATO quality

candidate_matches.label: sparse
candidate_matches.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being scattered; spread irregularly, and at a distance from each other.

candidate_matches.label: dispersed
candidate_matches.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being distributed or spread over a considerable extent.

candidate_matches.label: distributed
candidate_matches.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being spread out or scattered about or divided up.

candidate_matches.label: undistributed
candidate_matches.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being spread out or scattered about or divided up.

candidate_matches.label: neoplastic, metastatic
candidate_matches.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to spread and invade distant tissues.

candidate_matches.label: bitufted cell morphology
candidate_matches.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have two clusters of primary dendrites that originate directly and extend from opposite ends of the soma, and axons that form a plexus which spreads widely. Compared to the narrow vertical axonal projection of bipolar morphology, and the 'horse-tail' axonal cluster of double boquet cells, bitufted morphology has wide horizontal axonal spans, and branching that occur close to the soma. Their vertical projection is also less extensive and crosses mostly to neighboring layers.

occurrences.title: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics
occurrences.evidence_text: there is evidence that all eight species colonized new plots since the last round of monitoring... at least 25% of the currently occupied plots are a result of population spread
